messages.subject: [R-pkgs] New package: frite
messages.body_plain:
Hello,

I've released a new package called *frite* and it has been published by
cran.

Its purpose is to provide basic tools for code generation. Additionally,
there are also more general functions to aid in code refactoring.

You can download it here:
https://cran.r-project.org/package=frite

Thanks for reading,
Christopher Peralta

- *frite* author and maintainer

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-pkgs] New package : anyLib
messages.body_plain:
Hi,

This message is to announce the release of anyLib, a new package to install
easily any package from CRAN, Bioconductor or GitHub. It also automatically
loads the installed package. So do not bother anymore to remember where a
package come from and if you already installed it or not, just anyLib!

You can download it from https://cran.r-project.org/package=anyLib

Best,

Aur?lien Chateigner, PhD
Postdoctoral Fellow

BioForA, INRA, ONF, 45075, Orl?ans, France
INRA Val-de-Loire
2163 avenue de la Pomme de Pin
CS 40001 - Ardon
45075 ORLEANS CEDEX 2
France

https://www6.val-de-loire.inra.fr/uragpf/

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [R-pkgs] New package cleandata: functions to clean business data
messages.subject_clean: New package cleandata: functions to clean business data
messages.body_plain:
Hi useRs,

A new package, cleandata, has been released on CRAN.

This package contains functions to that work with data frame to inspect,
impute, and encode data. The functions for imputation and encoding can
produce log files to help you keep track of your process.

I designed the package to be efficient to work with business datasets.
However, it can also work with other datasets, especially datasets with
many columns.

A Demo of Using It to Wrangle Business Data:
https://rpubs.com/neilalien/rpkgcd010demo01

CRAN: https://cran.r-project.org/package=cleandata
GitHub: https://github.com/sherrisherry/cleandata

All the best,

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
